KPI-01.label: Total Open Vulnerabilities
KPI-01.description: Number of security controls (F-01..F-18) where at least one automated check failed or returned partial results. Lower is better — each count represents an area of the application that does not meet its stated security claim.

KPI-02.label: Critical & High Severity Open
KPI-02.description: Subset of open findings rated Critical or High priority (e.g. role tampering, IDOR, missing auth controls). These should be remediated before production release.

KPI-03.label: Access Control Failures
KPI-03.description: Count of failed AC-* checks: role tampering, unauthorized admin/employee access, IDOR on dashboard or transactions. Maps to OWASP A01 Broken Access Control.

KPI-04.label: Authentication Weaknesses
KPI-04.description: Failed checks related to passwords, session secrets, and login rate limiting (F-01, F-02, WC-02). Indicates risk of credential abuse or brute-force attacks.

KPI-05.label: SQL Injection Risk
KPI-05.description: Failed SI-* checks: unsafe SQL construction in code or exploitable query parameters on statements/transactions routes.

KPI-06.label: Forms Missing CSRF Protection
KPI-06.description: 1 if state-changing forms (transfer, profile, login) lack CSRF tokens; 0 if protected. Unprotected forms allow cross-site request forgery attacks.

KPI-07.label: IDOR & Data Exposure
KPI-07.description: Issues where one user can access another user's data via parameter tampering (e.g. ?user_id=). Direct indicator of insecure direct object references.

KPI-08.label: Audit Log Completeness
KPI-08.description: Percentage of critical security events (transfers, unauthorized access) logged with IP address, severity, and description. Required for forensics and SIEM correlation.

KPI-09.label: Failed Login Log Quality
KPI-09.description: Percentage of LOGIN_FAILED audit rows that include IP address and severity. Incomplete logs hinder intrusion detection.

KPI-10.label: Privacy Workflow Coverage
KPI-10.description: Count of GDPR privacy routes present (/privacy, export, delete, consent) out of 4. Missing routes indicate compliance gaps for data subject rights.

KPI-11.label: Security Headers Present
KPI-11.description: Count of required HTTP security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, etc.) detected on responses. Headers reduce XSS, clickjacking, and MITM risk.

KPI-12.label: Session Cookie Hardening
KPI-12.description: Count of secure session cookie flags enabled (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite, timeout). Weak cookies enable session hijacking.

KPI-13.label: Mean Remediation Age
KPI-13.description: Average days open for tracked findings in the remediation register. Used to measure program velocity.

KPI-14.label: Automated Test Pass Rate
KPI-14.description: Percentage of security smoke and transfer tests passing (smoke_tests.py, test_transfer_security.py). Validates regressions after fixes.

KPI-15.label: Overall Compliance Score
KPI-15.description: Weighted average of OWASP, ISO, NIST, and GDPR framework sub-scores derived from check results. Primary executive metric for program health.
